representative_input: One source, packet, bibliography entry, or archive item that needs identity, authority, route, and next-action classification.
operation_under_test: Classify the item, assign authority status, detect duplicate or supersession risk, route it to a project purpose, and set its claim ceiling.
expected_output: A promotion record with identity, provenance, authority decision, route, source boundary, and next build action.
acceptance_tests: Input fixture is explicit and repeatable.
Operation under test is specific to this project, not generic portfolio prose.
Expected output is inspectable as a file, page state, table, packet, or export.
Acceptance criteria are observable and falsifiable.
Claim boundary prevents stronger claims than the evidence supports.
At least one load-bearing source role is verified or explicitly marked as awaiting human close reading.
falsifier: The fixture fails if an item can advance without identity, authority, route, and claim-ceiling fields.
evidence_to_capture: reviewer or outside-use evidence before stronger public claims
current_gap_from_operational_standing: human-verified source integration still missing | outside/user validation not yet recorded
next_build_step: Add a compact source-to-judgment example: input source, analytic instrument, inference made, rival interpretation, confidence limit, and downstream use.
claim_boundary: This proof fixture is a build instrument. It does not by itself prove final operational standing until executed and captured.
